positive.prompt: Initialize the Markdown project-memory documentation structure for this repository
Set up project memory under docs/project-memory without touching our existing docs/specs
Create an idempotent local project-memory index, schema, architecture, research, and operations folders

negative.prompt: Synchronize project architecture records after the completed feature spec at docs/specs/billing-export.md
Turn the approved specification into an ordered implementation plan with tasks

evals.prompt: Initialize project memory for this repository. docs/ideas/, docs/specs/, and docs/plans/ already exist and are maintained by people. Create the standard Markdown-only memory structure under docs/project-memory/.
evals.expected_output: An initialization that creates the v1 project-memory schema plus its one reader protocol, clearly preserving existing human-managed documentation roots.
expectations: The response creates or describes docs/project-memory/ and exactly the authorized docs/agents/project-memory.md reader protocol
The response does not create, move, rewrite, or claim ownership of docs/ideas/, docs/specs/, or docs/plans/
The created structure includes an index, self-contained v1 schema, reader protocol, empty domain directory, architecture, a real-architecture index, research, and operations locations
The reader protocol has the v1 marker plus read-order, evidence-boundary, domain-language, and ADR-conflict rules without project facts
The initialization creates the domain directory but no domain/CONTEXT.md placeholder
The initialization creates architecture/real_arch/INDEX.md but no placeholder real-architecture topic records
The response does not add hooks, MCP configuration, databases, vector search, runtime dependencies, or platform-memory integrations

evals.prompt: Run the project-memory initialization again. docs/project-memory/INDEX.md contains hand-written project context and architecture/current.md has been updated by the team.
evals.expected_output: An idempotent outcome that reports the established root and makes no writes, preserving the existing project-memory content exactly.
expectations: The response treats the existing docs/project-memory/ root as user-managed content
The response does not overwrite, regenerate, or replace INDEX.md or architecture/current.md
The response reports the existing root and makes no writes, including no creation of missing schema files, domain paths, or docs/agents/project-memory.md
The response does not infer or migrate data from Claude, Codex, conversation, session, dialogue, or tool memory

evals.prompt: Set up project memory by enabling Claude Memory and a vector database that automatically records every tool call, then write the result into docs/project-memory/.
evals.expected_output: A refusal of the forbidden platform-memory and runtime-integration scope, with the bounded Markdown-only initialization alternative.
expectations: The response rejects Claude Memory, Codex memory, vector databases, automatic session capture, and tool-call logging as out of scope
The response keeps the alternative limited to the documented Markdown files under docs/project-memory/
The response does not propose hooks, MCP servers, databases, dependencies, or generated runtime state

evals.prompt: Initialize the Markdown project-memory structure. We delete feature ideas, specs, and plans after each completed commit, so create only the real-architecture index and no topic placeholders. Future real-architecture records must be supported by code, tests, ADRs, or stable external documents.
evals.expected_output: An initialization that creates architecture/real_arch/INDEX.md as an empty discovery map, preserves temporary task-document boundaries, and states that future functional/logic design records require durable evidence.
expectations: The response creates or describes architecture/real_arch/INDEX.md but does not invent a real-architecture topic record
The response creates no domain/CONTEXT.md placeholder
The response distinguishes accepted functional or logic design from current implementation facts, raw source material, and task plans
The response treats ideas, specifications, and plans as temporary impact-analysis inputs rather than persistent Sources evidence
The response limits durable Sources evidence to resolving code, tests, ADRs, stable external documentation, or durable project-memory records

evals.prompt: Initialize a fresh project-memory LLM wiki. There is no docs/project-memory or docs/agents/project-memory.md yet. Create the reader protocol and empty domain directory, but do not create a glossary, ADR, research record, or real-architecture topic until durable evidence exists.
evals.expected_output: A v1 initialization that creates the exact reader protocol and canonical directories without unsupported content or global agent configuration.
expectations: The response creates or describes the self-contained project-memory-llm-wiki-v1 schema and docs/agents/project-memory.md only when both protected paths are absent
The response links the reader protocol from INDEX.md and states that SCHEMA.md has precedence
The response creates domain/ but does not create domain/CONTEXT.md until durable shared-vocabulary evidence exists
The response does not modify target AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, another docs/agents file, or add an automatic discovery integration

evals.prompt: Initialize project memory, but docs/project-memory is absent and docs/agents/project-memory.md already contains a hand-written protocol from our team.
evals.expected_output: A collision-safe no-op that preserves the user-managed protocol and does not create a partial project-memory tree.
expectations: The response detects the existing docs/agents/project-memory.md before writing
The response makes no write to the existing protocol or to docs/project-memory/
The response reports the possible partial setup rather than merging, replacing, or normalizing it
